title: games like evangelion
wikitext:
[]: games like evangelion             Quote Post by FatFatus » Thu Mar 23, 2023 10:21 am games that make you feel like watching eva agin

[]: Re: games like evangelion             Quote Post by Blockio » Thu Mar 23, 2023 11:50 am Sort of depends on the aspect that you're looking for; as far as overall atmosphere goes, while it is not the same by any stretch, Signalis hits a lot of similar notes (and is just generally excellent!)

[]: Re: games like evangelion             Quote Post by FatFatus » Mon Mar 27, 2023 1:09 pm Thx, might check it out.

[]: Re: games like evangelion             Quote Post by Osoa » Fri Oct 20, 2023 5:32 am NieR Automata shares many themes to Evangelion and has many references to it

[]: Re: games like evangelion             Quote Post by Blockio » Fri Oct 20, 2023 6:46 am I've actually played something recently that does fit the bill: Heaven Will Be Mine . It's a visual novel that covers a lot of similar ground as the more esoteric half of NGE specifically; mechs being extensions of the self, mild existential shenanigans and the sex-laden violence that 01 likes to indulge in when she goes berserk

[]: Re: games like evangelion             Quote Post by The Killer of Heroes » Fri Oct 20, 2023 8:55 am It's not perfect or anything, but I'll recommend another visual novel in Chaos;Head. The protagonist of that game actually is the twerp that people think Shinji is for not "getting in the damn robot".

[]: Re: games like evangelion             Quote Post by Turismo030650 » Fri Oct 20, 2023 9:33 am FatFatus wrote: games that make you feel like watching eva agin

[]: Re: games like evangelion             Quote Post by Blockio » Fri Oct 20, 2023 5:37 pm No. Girlfriend of Steel would feel at home during the action episodes, but it was very much made after the fact

[]: Re: games like evangelion             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Sat Oct 21, 2023 8:03 pm A long time ago I read something about Takeda basically reusing dropped ideas from NGE into GoS (which might be on Evangelion Original), and wanting to outsize Mana and introduce her into NGE canon. You can find some older magazines that introduced Mana as straight-up "Shinji's girlfriend". Takeda seemingly envisioned Mana as a new potential cash cow. I could never find that source again and apparently, it was never translated, so don't take my word for it. Of course, this idea never took off either way, so you can safely put GoS as not canon just like SIRP and all those other silly little Eva games. On topic though, a lot of people like linking MGS2 to Eva. We know Kojima is at least a casual Eva fan. Something else I played a while ago was this indie game called Into The Breach, a turn-based tactics game by the creators of FTL. Interestingly, you had a specific goal to protect civilian buildings from the alien monsters as those provided power to your mechs, an additional health bar of sorts. Kinda reminded me of Tokyo-3. Oh, and theres's an Eva MUGEN game that's pretty damn complete. https://gamebanana.com/mods/47694

[]: Re: games like evangelion             Quote Post by Turismo030650 » Mon Oct 23, 2023 4:36 pm FelipeFritschF wrote: A long time ago I read something about Takeda basically reusing dropped ideas from NGE into GoS (which might be on Evangelion Original), and wanting to outsize Mana and introduce her into NGE canon. You can find some older magazines that introduced Mana as straight-up "Shinji's girlfriend". Takeda seemingly envisioned Mana as a new potential cash cow. I could never find that source again and apparently, it was never translated, so don't take my word for it. Of course, this idea never took off either way, so you can safely put GoS as not canon just like SIRP and all those other silly little Eva games. On topic though, a lot of people like linking MGS2 to Eva. We know Kojima is at least a casual Eva fan. Something else I played a while ago was this indie game called Into The Breach, a turn-based tactics game by the creators of FTL. Interestingly, you had a specific goal to protect civilian buildings from the alien monsters as those provided power to your mechs, an additional health bar of sorts. Kinda reminded me of Tokyo-3.

[]: Re: games like evangelion             Quote Post by MoonRabbitMallow » Mon Oct 23, 2023 8:57 pm Oh, games similar to Eva? Or at least, games an Eva fan would enjoy? I would personally recommend SOMA for Evangelion fans. They don't have too many similarities aside from being sci-fi with a heavy emphasis on robots and mechanics, and being very existential, but an Eva fan can get a total kick out of it! (Actually, that kind of sounds exactly like Eva. They cover similar topics so honestly the more I think about it, the more similar they are in my brain.) It's a survival horror by the folks who made Amnesia: The Dark Descent about surviving an underwater facility overrun with strange creatures and questionable AI ethics. Not sure how high it ranks on the scare factor since RPGHorror is more my niche and those games don't tend to be the scariest, but it is an experience worth checking out, I would recommend that one for people who liked the more identity and existential aspects of Eva, but a science fiction horror nerd might like it too. The more I think about it, the more the game seems similar to Eva in many ways, from both the sci-fi aspects to the existential ones. I know OP wasn't asking for recommendations per se, but I do recommend it. It really threw me for a loop similar to how Eva did. Now I kinda wanna watch Eva again and play SOMA...

[]: Re: games like evangelion             Quote Post by Gendos Glasses » Thu Jul 18, 2024 8:03 pm I can recommend metal gear solid 1-4

[]: Re: games like evangelion             Quote Post by Clowncarcrash » Sat Jul 20, 2024 5:59 am Absolutely shocked no one mentioned Xenogears. Anime styled game, mechs, tons of reference to the Christian mythos and philosophy. It came out in the 90s as well so I get the exact same kinda vibes from it.

[]: Re: games like evangelion             Quote Post by The Killer of Heroes » Sun Jul 21, 2024 11:54 am The problem with Xenogears is that while the story is cool, like a lot of 90's Squaresoft stuff the gameplay has aged like milk.
